title: Syracuse City Schools spend 50% more per student yet rank near bottom in state test scores
summary: Syracuse City School District spends $31,333 per student (50% above national average of $20,000) yet ranks 644th of 658 districts in ELA proficiency and 549th of 550 in math proficiency. Leadership transition underway with Deputy Superintendent Pamela Odom assuming superintendent role; district considering $125,000 consulting contract with outgoing Superintendent Anthony Davis's company.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Education

political_signal.description: Media investigation revealing significant resource-to-outcome disparity; potential governance concerns regarding leadership transition and consulting contract with departing superintendent's company.

processing_notes: Article contains critical performance data; math proficiency state appears to have typo (54* vs 54%)
Consulting contract timing sensitive—coincides with superintendent transition
Cost-per-student figure ($31,333) provides benchmarking opportunity against state/national comparators
No direct City of Syracuse government connection evident; district is separate entity, but may impact municipal budget/tax discussions
Data source: New York State; recommend cross-referencing with state education department records
